blocks.content: Shirone and Rian jumped down.
The slowly rising priest opened his eyes wide.
It was impossible for a human to lift their eyelids while their pupils were slashed.

blocks.speaker: Priest (Oracle)
blocks.content: No way… the High Judge of Teraphos?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Yes. I'm borrowing this body to deliver my voice. Humans call it an oracle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can you possess anyone?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Not exactly. You need to connect to the Teraphos channel, which is only possible in a trance state. Usually, dreams are used. But this guy showed no signs of entering a trance.
Shirone looked at the priest with pity.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: The stronger the channeler, the higher the density of the mind they can transmit. Among humans, Maylay would be the most superior, but even someone like this can manage small talk.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So… you came here for small talk?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was the High Judge of Teraphos living such a boring life?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: I came to let you know that any human connected to the Teraphos channel can communicate with me at any time. This human's channeling was too weak, so it only succeeded at the brink of death, but a powerful channeler could receive my entire mind.
Shirone recalled the execution of the Avalokitesvara Annihilation through Maylay.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You should've said that sooner.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: The council just approved it. Also, this means the Teraphos species have recognized you as humanity's representative.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm.
As Shirone fell into thought, a groan echoed.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Ah, ahhh…
Emma, still unable to move, was curled in a corner, trembling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What about her? Can she channel?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Her spiritual senses are too weak. She wouldn't hear my voice even in her sleep. The only one here capable of channeling is this human.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if she's pathetic, she's still a mage, huh?
Emma clasped her hands and asked,

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Are you… really the god of Teraphos?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: We don't have a word for 'god,' but I am what humans call one.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Then… you know everything about me?

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Your name is Emma. The biological partner of Adoks, a centurion in the Kazura border guard. But by human standards, you betrayed your vows and had biological relations with Miguelan, Adoks' subordinate, 63 times over a month. Recently, you even planned to poison Adoks—

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Stop! Stop it!
The horror of having her secrets exposed was overwhelming.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Please forgive me! I'll live righteously from now on! My husband drinks all the time, breaks things, and hits me when he comes home… And he was the one who cheated first! With that 38-year-old bar hostess!
As Emma's eyes filled with venom again, Kido clicked his tongue.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: This household is a mess. Well, life's like that.
Teraphos had classified Emma as evil, but that was because they observed humans from outside their lives.
Once you step onto the stage of life, only the emotions of the moment feel vivid, and it's impossible to tell what came first.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: I am not a being who forgives.
Emma was half out of her mind.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: I'm sorry! I'm really sorry! Please forgive me. If my husband finds out I came here, I'll die!
Shirone reassured her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't worry. I won't tell anyone. But… could you let us stay at your place for a few days?

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, yes! If that will atone for my sins…
She seemed to mistakenly believe Shirone had the authority to forgive her sins.
Of course, after witnessing the miracle of Teraphos standing there with slashed pupils, it was a natural reaction.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What's the plan?
When Rian asked, Shirone turned to the High Judge and said,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tell the king of Kazura, Orkamp, that I've arrived. Through an oracle.
Kido clapped his hands.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Aha. That's one way.
A sort of communication satellite.
If Shirone's words were received from space and transmitted to the faithful, they could deliver the message to any country in the world.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Is that so? Wait a moment.
The High Judge closed his eyes.
The locations of all Teraphos faithful around the world flashed in his mind.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: There aren't many Teraphos channels in Kazura. Even if there are, most are low-level like this one.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So it won't work.
Each faithful was like a relay station, but compared to humanity's numbers, they were few.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: I found the closest channel. There's one Teraphos faithful within a kilometer of Kazura's royal palace.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Within a kilometer… A bureaucrat?
The higher the rank, the more convenient it would be.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: A military officer serving in the royal guard. He keeps his faith in Teraphos a secret from everyone but his family.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's fine. They'll have no choice but to believe. First, send the location of Emma's house. Say that Shirone requests an audience. And…
Shirone chose his words carefully.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tell him I have something to say about his son.
The High Judge's eyelids trembled slightly, and that was all.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Transmission complete. The oracle will be received tonight after he falls asleep. It should be delivered by dawn tomorrow.
It was an incredible time-saver, but Kido suggested an easier method.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Why not just possess the officer like you did now? Write it down on paper and leave.

blocks.speaker: High Judge (Oracle)
blocks.content: Forced trance states disrupt order. Only humanity's representative, the Hexa, can summon me.
Shirone nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it. Then I'll wait for the palace's response.
With that, the communication ended.
As the High Judge's expression faded, the fake priest covered his eyes and screamed.

blocks.speaker: Priest
blocks.content: Aaaah! It hurts! It hurts! Where am I?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He doesn't remember.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maylay had been the same.
Those whose legs had been severed had already died from excessive blood loss, so escaping the cave without sight would be difficult.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Spirit Zone. But is it possible in this state?
Rian placed a hand on Shirone's shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Let's get out of here, Shirone. Staying longer won't do any good.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …Right.
As Shirone's group followed Emma out of the cave, the fake cult leader's screams echoed behind them.
Γùå Γùå Γùå
After descending the mountain while avoiding the border guards, Shirone could gauge Kazura's current situation.
The number of soldiers had been reinforced, but their morale had weakened, with some even napping sporadically.
The king's authority had eroded, and their self-esteem had hit rock bottom.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: That's the village.
It was a village of about 40 households, with all buildings made of wood except for the government office.
Looking down at the village illuminated by torches, Kido said,

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I think I'd rather stay in the mountains.
In a city, it might be different, but to the rural villagers, a goblin was just a monster.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you sure? It might take up to two days if things run late.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Heh, what are you talking about? Goblins aren't as weak as humans.
Shirone and Rian were superhumans who had trained relentlessly, but most people couldn't match a goblin's physical abilities.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: The mountains are more comfortable. It's easier to find food, too.
Human food wasn't bad, but the taste of blood-soaked raw meat was irresistible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry. I'll send a signal when someone arrives from the palace.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It's fine.
Kido spun his spear and vanished from sight in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Kido won't get caught by the guards.
As Shirone finished his thought, Emma nervously spoke up.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Um, Your Holiness, what should I tell my husband?
Bringing two strange young men into the house would enrage him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Introduce us as merchants. We can store our weapons in rings. And we'll pay for your hospitality.
Seeing the pouch of gold coins, Emma sighed in relief.
Money wasn't her priority, but her husband Adoks would sell his own family for it.
As they passed the wooden fence, chickens flapped their wings, and dogs barked at the strangers' scent.
Arriving at the thirteenth house on the village's east side, Emma unlocked the door and turned to Shirone's group.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Come in.
The 14-pyeong space had no separation between the kitchen and bedroom, with a staircase leading to the second floor in the corner.
Adoks, sitting at the table, snapped at her.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Where the hell have you been?
Emma's shoulders tensed at the sight of her husband, who was unusually home today.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Honey, you're back already?
Adoks was a rough-bearded man in his mid-30s.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: It's been dark for ages, and you're spouting nonsense? Do I look like some drunkard who drinks all day?
With a hangover-induced stomachache, Adoks scowled and asked Shirone and Rian,

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Who are you? Never seen you before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We're merchants. Could we stay here for two days?

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: What kind of merchant comes to this backwater? Are you… heretics? Be honest.
Reports had come in that the Teraphos cult was approaching nearby villages to extort money.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha, of course not. We're from a new merchant group looking for places to deliver goods. We'll just survey the market for a day and leave.
Such things weren't unheard of.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: What merchant group?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We'd rather not say. This business is sensitive to rumors…

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Are you kidding me?
Shirone hastily added,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Neid Merchant Group. Please keep it a secret. Emma told us you're influential here…
When he took out a gold coin, Adoks' pupils shook.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: That much money…

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Misah, the bar hostess, would faint with joy.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: They're just trying to butter me up, huh?
But as a soldier, taking money outright could cause trouble later.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: The hardware store across the street also takes lodgers. Why not go there? It's just an attic room, so it's no different from here.
Shirone understood Adoks' psychology.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hehe, staying at a store would draw attention from other merchants. You know how this business is.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Even so, this amount is too much.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's only right to pay more for the inconvenience. Think of it as a merchant's sincerity.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Well, I guess so.
Adoks took the gold coin from Shirone and confirmed it was the international standard issued by the Red Line Currency Bureau.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Fine. Treat them well, make sure they're comfortable. I'll be staying out tonight.
Emma could guess where he was going, but today, she had no choice but to let him go.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Yes, come home early.
Despite her gentle tone, Emma gritted her teeth the moment Adoks left.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: That bastard. He's going to that hostess again. He'll flaunt that gold coin and show off.
There was nothing to say to comfort her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's eat first. There should be some alcohol, too.
Perhaps unable to stay sober, Emma downed the remaining half-bottle of alcohol.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Honestly, that Misah is only better than me at entertaining men! My husband's an idiot, but that woman's worse!
As Emma ranted, Shirone listened without showing annoyance.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Sigh, I wish my husband was half as good as you, Your Holiness.
When she drunkenly slumped onto the table, Rian carried her to bed.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Are you sure about this, Shirone? Wouldn't it be better to wait at an inn?
The fact that Emma's husband was a centurion in the border guard was concerning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Adoks is fine. He can't file an international lawsuit based on speculation. The real risk is Emma.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Right. If Emma talks, things will spiral out of control. That's why you told the High Judge we'd stay here.
Because he couldn't bring himself to kill Emma.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. Until someone arrives from Kazura's palace, we can't let our guard down. If there's even a hint she might talk…

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Then what?
Shirone's gaze turned cold.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then I'll leave it to you.
Mages were cold-hearted people.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: …Got it.
Shirone wanted to eliminate the risk before it became uncontrollable, yet he hadn't abandoned Emma in the cave.
He wanted to find a path where everyone could be happy.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Walking the tightrope between ideals and reality. Is that what it means to be a mage?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Like Jane, who had to claw at her own heart to save Aria.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Life is really hard for them.
Rian thought he could never become a mage, even in another life.
Γùå Γùå Γùå
The next morning.
Adoks left for work, rubbing his stomach.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Lieutenant! Miguelan!
Miguelan opened the door and entered.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: You called, sir?
Unaware that Miguelan was his wife's lover, Adoks pretended to sip from a cup and said,

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: You still have some honey, right? Mix it in and bring it to me.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Did you drink again?
Adoks had sworn off alcohol yesterday and left work early.
After gulping down the honey water, Adoks wiped his beard and said,

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Ah, I feel alive now.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: You went to Misah, didn't you?

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Heh, shut up. I hit the jackpot yesterday. It was an amazing night.
As Adoks reminisced, his expression darkened.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Oh, and you, go check on my house.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: What's wrong?

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Something's off. Some merchants came yesterday, and I think they're up to something.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Are they heretics?

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Hey, have you ever seen heretics spend money? Ah, no.
Adoks hastily shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Anyway, check it out. I was out last night. See if anything happened with Emma.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: What a piece of trash.
While he partied with a bar hostess, he sent his lieutenant to spy on his wife.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Understood.
Seeing Emma would be a bonus, so Miguelan rushed to Adoks' house.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: That guy's suspicious.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: The so-called merchants looked noble enough to be believed, and they were even handsome.
Emma opened the door with a startled expression, but Miguelan kept his composure.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Miguelan? What's wrong?

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: The centurion sent me. He has an urgent message.
He wanted to say he loved her.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: You have guests. Sorry, but could you give us a moment?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We'll be upstairs.
As Shirone and Rian went up the stairs, Miguelan couldn't hold back and hugged Emma.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: I missed you, Emma.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: Why did you come now? What if we're caught?

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Your husband sent me. That bastard. He spent the night with Misah again. Let's go through with the plan we talked about.
The plan to poison Adoks.

blocks.speaker: Emma
blocks.content: I can't. If we're caught…

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Don't worry. No one cares if someone dies in this backwater.
Terrified at the thought of the gods listening, Emma hesitated.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Trust me. We'll see this through together.
Miguelan found her even more endearing and leaned in for a kiss, but the door suddenly opened as Adoks entered.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Emma! Those merchants who were here— Huh?
Miguelan and Emma quickly pulled apart, but it was too late.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: What? Why are you holding my wife's shoulder?

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: S-sir, why are you home?
The gold coin Shirone had given him was the problem.
Most traveling merchants used the international standard, but Kazura's borders had been closed for over a year.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: They might really be heretics.
But now, Adoks' mind was filled only with rage toward Miguelan.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: How dare you touch my wife!
As his superior drew his sword, Miguelan raised his hands.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Sir! It's a misunderstanding!

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Misunderstanding? What misunderstanding? Was there honey on my wife's shoulder?

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Th-that's…!
Miguelan's mind raced.

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: The merchants! They tried to assault your wife!

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: What nonsense!

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: Why would I lie? I was just calming your terrified wife.
Adoks' eyes narrowed with murderous intent.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: …Where are those bastards now?

blocks.speaker: Miguelan
blocks.content: They're barricaded on the second floor. I blocked the exit, so they're trapped.
Miguelan planned to escape with Emma if Adoks rushed upstairs, but things didn't go as planned.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: You damn brats! Get down here now! I'll slice you alive and eat you!
The house shook from his roaring.
Shirone and Rian calmly descended the stairs.
Unlike yesterday, Rian now carried a massive greatsword on his back, freezing Adoks' face.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Wh-what the hell are you?
Rian gripped the greatsword's hilt and asked,

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, is it time?
If Emma talked, Adoks and Miguelan would have to die too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is there no other way?
Then someone knocked loudly on the door.

blocks.speaker: Knocker
blocks.content: Is anyone home? I'm looking for someone.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: I'm busy! Get lost!
The door swung open despite the rude dismissal, and a giant old man strode in.

blocks.speaker: Old Man
blocks.content: You little brat, are you ignoring me— Huh?
The moment Adoks saw the emblem on the old man's armor, his face turned pale, as did Miguelan's.

blocks.speaker: Adoks
blocks.content: Th-the royal guard?
Sensing the tense atmosphere, the old man scanned the room and locked eyes with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ritni Walker
blocks.content: Long time no see, Arian Shirone.
He had expected a quick route due to the urgency, but this was faster than anticipated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I knew it'd be him.
Shirone, having reined in his killing intent, smiled at the familiar face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. It's been a while.
The captain of Kazura's royal guard, Ritni Walker.
